meta.title: NeuroAttention Lab — Operating System for Human Attention
meta.description: NeuroAttention Lab — a science-based method for nervous system regulation and attention development, XR platform, research laboratory, and DOM Unity community.
hero.title: The world has entered a new era
hero.sub: And humans can't keep up with its pace.
hero.desc: Today, the ability to adapt to change is becoming the primary condition for health and quality of life.
problem.title: Over the past 30 years, the technological leap has radically transformed our lives
problem.subtitle: creating a burden that the human body was never prepared for through the course of evolution. This has led to massive consequences on every level.
problem.individual.title: Individual consequences
problem.social.title: Social consequences
problem.social.1: Reduced societal resilience to stressful events
problem.social.2: Deterioration in the quality of communication
facts.title: The scale of changes we are not prepared for
facts.1: The information load has increased tenfold: from books and television — to the around-the-clock stream of internet, social media, and notifications.
facts.2: The number of people living outside their home country has doubled over recent decades — to more than 300 million.
facts.3: Where once a single profession could accompany a person for life, today the average worker changes between 5 and 7 career paths.
facts.4: Family models have also changed: not long ago, marriage lasted a lifetime, yet today nearly half of all unions dissolve under the pressure of modern trends.
facts.conclusion: Humans urgently need new tools for adaptation.
solution.card1: At the root of all the problems listed above lies the loss of control over attention and the state of the nervous system.
solution.card2: Attention is the central function of the brain, uniting cognitive, emotional, and bodily processes.
solution.card3: That is why developing attention becomes the fundamental tool for human adaptation to the modern world.
method.title: We have developed a science-based method for nervous system regulation and attention development — NeuroAttention.
method.rehab_text: Rehabilitation Program — restoration of cognitive functions in cases of nervous system impairments.
xr.title: XR NeuroAttention Platform
xr.desc: To enable users to apply NeuroAttention protocols, an XR platform for virtual and augmented reality is being developed.
xr.subtitle: The platform enables you to:
xr.feature1: Undergo diagnostics of the nervous system's condition
xr.feature3: See and influence the body's physiological signals in real time
xr.feature4: Progress through development levels via a training and achievement system
xr.feature5: Grow alongside other users in shared XR training spaces
lab.title: NeuroAttention Lab
lab.desc: A research platform built on a personalized operating system for human attention. We study the mechanisms of attention, states, and behavior through neuroscience, technology, and practical protocols. We create environments and tools for human adaptation and development in a technological world.
dom.intro: The entire project structure is being built to address that very problem:
dom.title: Human adaptation to the new era.
dom.desc1: This concerns every one of us — regardless of status, profession, or country.
dom.desc2: Alone, under the current environmental conditions, the human body cannot cope with these changes.
dom.card_title: That is why DOM Unity was born
dom.card_desc: A community of people united to shape a new online and offline environment that fosters adaptation to a rapidly changing world and the development of human potential through technology and collaborative learning.
team.nick_desc1: Creator of the NeuroAttention Method. Researcher in attention and self-regulation with 12+ years of method development.
team.nick_desc2: Trained 500+ people and established first international clients in the US, Asia, and Europe.
team.team_title: At the core of the DOM Unity community is the team behind the project.
team.takhir_desc: Leads XR platform architecture, technology development, and training systems.
team.sia_desc: Designs NeuroAttention environments integrating aesthetics, perception, and therapeutic impact.
team.yegor_desc: Leads content production, media systems, and brand expression.
team.support: The project is also supported by a team of specialists and partners in research, technology, and media.
eco.summary: All of them are part of a single ecosystem — DOM
eco.desc: An open ecosystem uniting technology, research, and a community of people working on the development of human potential.
eco.collab5: People who share the project's vision
p.meta.title: NeuroAttention Programs — Attention Training Protocols
p.meta.description: NeuroAttention programs: self-guided, personalized, group, rehabilitation, and professional training.
p.hero.title: NeuroAttention Programs
p.hero.subtitle: Attention training and nervous system adaptation protocols
p.hero.desc: Choose your format of participation: from self-guided work to deep personal transformation and professional training
p.xr.title: NeuroAttention XR
p.xr.subtitle: The next stage of the method's evolution — attention and state management in an immersive environment with biometric feedback
p.xr.desc1: The XR platform brings NeuroAttention protocols into a digital environment where attention becomes measurable, manageable, and trainable in real time.
p.xr.desc2: This direction is currently in development and forms the foundation of the entire future system.
p.os.title: Personal operating system for attention management
p.os.subtitle: Every program participant receives an interface where a visual dynamic model of your personality is formed.
p.os.desc: NeuroMap grows with you as you progress through the program and reflects the real dynamics of your attention: how emotions trigger thoughts, how thoughts shape actions, and how environmental events influence the entire chain. The map connects all of this into a unified system of observation and control.
p.programs.title: NeuroAttention Method Programs
p.programs.subtitle: Long-term work with attention and state, aimed at real improvement in quality of life and adaptation to the demands of the modern environment
p.self.title: Self-Guided Program
p.self.desc: An independent program following NeuroAttention protocols. Includes structured work with attention, body, and state through practices, theory, and assignments.
p.self.f2: Access to attention protocols and practices
p.self.f6: Access to the DOM Unity community
p.self.result: Restoring basic control over attention and state
p.guided.title: Guided Program
p.guided.desc: Deep work with the NeuroAttention Method alongside a personal specialist. Includes everything from Self-Guided + individual guidance.
p.guided.f1: NeuroMap + live feedback from a specialist
p.guided.f4: Work with traumatic and behavioral patterns
p.guided.result: Faster and more lasting changes in state and behavior
p.group.title: Group Program
p.group.desc: Group NeuroAttention experience in a facilitated format. Includes collective practices, group dynamics, and a shared environment for growth.
p.group.f2: Weekly group practices and training sessions
p.group.f6: Access to the DOM Unity community and events
p.group.result: Accelerated progress through structure and environment
p.rehab.title: Rehabilitation Program
p.rehab.desc: A program for the restoration of cognitive functions and quality of life in cases of physiological nervous system impairments.
p.rehab.f1: Personal work with a specialist only
p.rehab.f3: Coordination with the patient's medical team
p.rehab.result: Support for the restoration of cognitive functions and attention control
p.spec.title: Specialist Training
p.spec.subtitle: Training to work with the NeuroAttention Method for integration into professional practice
p.spec.l1_desc: Understanding attention, the nervous system, and basic protocols
p.spec.l2_desc: Working with people, conducting practices, diagnostics
p.spec.l3_desc: Full preparation for independent work and method transmission
p.research.title: Research Program
p.research.desc1: Every NeuroAttention program is also a source of structured data on human attention, state, and behavior.
p.research.desc2: Participating in a program is simultaneously work on yourself and a contribution to the science of attention. Your journey helps us understand how adaptation works and what makes the method effective.
p.research.footnote: All data is collected in a de-identified form and used exclusively for research purposes.
p.research.desc3: Participation in NeuroAttention Lab research is an opportunity to become part of one of the first systematic studies of attention.
p.cta.title: Not sure where to start?
p.cta.subtitle: Take the diagnostic test — we'll assess your current state and recommend the optimal format
m.meta.title: NeuroAttention Method — Attention Training and Self-Regulation
m.hero.title: Attention is your greatest advantage
m.hero.desc: In a world where everyone has access to knowledge, the advantage is created not by information, but by the ability to manage yourself.
m.hero.subtitle: NeuroAttention is a method that restores control over attention, state, and actions.
m.why.title: The central function that governs everything
m.why.desc: Attention cannot be reduced to 'concentration.' It is the fundamental mechanism of the brain that determines which signals are processed, which decisions are made, and how the body responds to the environment. Everything you do begins with attention.
m.why.c1_desc: What we notice and what we miss determines the quality of our decisions and reactions
m.why.c2_desc: How intensely we respond to stress depends on attention management
m.why.c3_desc: Effectiveness in action is a direct function of the ability to maintain focus
m.why.c4_desc: The quality and speed of choices are determined by where attention is directed
m.how.title: Three training directions
m.how.desc: The method is based on neuroplasticity — the brain's ability to change through targeted practice. Training proceeds along three axes:
m.how.d1_desc: Focus, switching, distribution — the formal functions of attention on which cognitive performance and behavior depend.
m.how.d2_desc: Perception of the body and space — interoception and proprioception as the foundation of emotional regulation and resilience.
m.how.d3_desc: Self-awareness and self-regulation — conscious management of stress, energy, and internal state.
m.skills.title: Core attention skills
m.s1.title: Focus and sustained attention
m.s1.desc: The ability to maintain stable concentration on a chosen object without involuntary switching. Developing this skill reduces distractibility and deepens information processing.
m.s1.brain: Brain zones: dorsolateral prefrontal cortex, anterior cingulate cortex
m.s2.title: Flexibility and switching
m.s2.desc: The ability to quickly redirect attention from one object to another without losing clarity. Determines reaction speed and adaptability in a multitasking environment.
m.s2.brain: Brain zones: frontoparietal networks, working memory
m.s3.title: Divided attention
m.s3.desc: Maintaining two or more points of attention simultaneously. Develops coordination between processes and the ability to manage multiple perceptual channels.
m.s3.brain: Brain zones: interhemispheric coordination, corpus callosum
m.s4.title: Volumetric perception
m.s4.desc: The transition from 'flat' body perception to volumetric. Develops three-dimensional awareness of bodily processes and increases the precision of internal observation.
m.s4.brain: Brain zones: insular cortex, cingulate cortex
m.s5.title: Interoception and proprioception
m.s5.desc: Integration of the internal body map with the proprioceptive map of movements and positions. Enhances coordination, resilience, and the ability to manage state through the body.
m.s6.title: Sensation recognition
m.s6.desc: Distinguishing types of sensations — skin, muscles, organs, breathing, heart. Allows for more precise state diagnostics and faster identification of the regulation point.
m.s6.brain: Brain zones: limbic system, insular cortex
m.s7.title: Self-regulation through attention
m.s7.desc: Conscious alteration of physiological states — stress, relaxation, activation — through directed attention to the body and breathing.
m.s7.brain: Brain zones: autonomic nervous system, vagus nerve
m.modules.title: Tools for enhancing the method
m.mod1.title: Breathing practices
m.mod1.desc: Regulation of the autonomic nervous system through conscious breathing
m.mod2.title: Movement and coordination
m.mod2.desc: Micro-motor work, interhemispheric coordination, tension reduction
m.mod3.title: Sound production
m.mod3.desc: Activation of respiratory and vibrational self-regulation channels
m.results.title: Practical effects of the method
m.results.desc: Attention training triggers changes on multiple levels. Below are the main areas where participants report measurable effects.
m.r1.title: Cognitive-emotional
m.r1.desc: Reduction of stress and anxiety, restoration of energy and concentration, emotion management and return of clarity.
m.r2.title: Personal and existential
m.r2.desc: Work with addictions and impulsivity, transformation of traumatic patterns, finding direction and new wholeness.
m.r3.title: Physical and clinical
m.r3.desc: Neurodegeneration prevention, support for ADHD and hyperactivity, restoration of sleep and circadian rhythms.
m.r4.title: Social and interpersonal
m.r4.desc: Increased effectiveness and quality of life, development of empathy and communication quality, strengthening of relationships.
m.audience.title: Who NeuroAttention is for
m.aud1.title: People under stress and burnout
m.aud1.desc: Chronic stress, anxiety, emotional instability, the feeling of 'burnout' — NeuroAttention restores control and resilience.
m.aud2.title: Professionals and students
m.aud2.desc: Productivity, mental clarity, learning speed — all of these functions depend on attention management.
m.aud3.title: Athletes and creatives
m.aud3.desc: Coordination, resilience, expanded perceptual range — NeuroAttention helps push beyond habitual limitations.
m.aud4.title: Psychotherapists and coaches
m.aud4.desc: An additional tool for working with trauma, addictions, psychosomatics, and the client's emotional regulation.
m.aud5.title: Parents and children
m.aud5.desc: Development of concentration, self-regulation, and psychological resilience from an early age.
m.aud6.title: Rehabilitation and clinical
m.aud6.desc: Recovery after strokes, neurodegenerative diseases, ADHD, and other forms of cognitive function impairment.
m.article.title: The article "NeuroAttention"
m.article.desc: A detailed description of the method, seven attention skills, and the scientific foundations of the approach.
m.final.title: Ready to start?
m.final.subtitle: Take the diagnostic test or explore the available training programs.

d.meta.title: DOM — Ecosystem for Unlocking Human Potential
d.hero.title: From adaptation to conscious participation
d.hero.subtitle: From Homo sapiens to Homo conscious
d.hero.desc: DOM — an ecosystem for unlocking human potential
d.idea.title: Core Idea
d.idea.desc: A human being is capable of consciously participating in their own evolution and, consequently, in the evolution of society. To do so, they need to take control of attention, state, and choice.
d.what.title: What this means
d.what.desc: DOM is an ecosystem where this becomes possible in practice, where a person stops living as a reaction to the environment and begins to consciously shape their state, decisions, and life direction.
d.is.title: DOM is
d.c1.title: NeuroAttention
d.c1.desc: A system for developing attention and the nervous system as the fundamental function for managing state and life
d.c2.title: XR NeuroAttention Platform
d.c2.desc: An environment for practice, learning, and interaction, including state work through neurofeedback
d.c3.title: NeuroAttention Lab
d.c3.desc: Diagnostics, experiments, and solution development. Physical laboratories and research centers
d.c4.title: DOM Custom Group
d.c4.desc: A marketplace of products, solutions, and life forms created within the DOM ecosystem
d.c5.title: DOM Unity
d.c5.desc: A community of people moving in the same direction — growth, awareness, and improvement of quality of life. Here the environment becomes a factor of growth.
d.for.title: Who DOM is for
d.f1.desc: Who feel the overload of the environment, instability and loss of control, and want to develop faster, more consciously, and more resiliently
d.f2.desc: Psychologists, coaches, neuro and body practitioners who want to deepen their work with attention and state
d.f3.desc: For whom it is important to maintain clarity, resilience, and quality of decisions amid constant change
d.f4.desc: Those who study the human being, consciousness, and the possibilities of development
d.f5.desc: That want to increase the resilience, effectiveness, and adaptability of their teams
d.f6.category: For systemic players and large organizations
d.f6.desc: Government and corporate structures seeking systemic approaches to human capital development and adaptability at scale
d.principle: Internal state shapes external reality. DOM creates an environment where this process becomes manageable.
d.join.title: Enter the system
t.meta.title: Adaptation Test — NeuroAttention Lab
t.meta.description: Test your adaptation to global change. Discover your adaptation profile and get personalized recommendations.
t.header.title: Adaptation Test
t.header.question: Question
t.intro.welcome: Welcome to the NeuroAttention adaptation test. I'll help you discover your adaptation profile.
t.intro.instruction: We'll start with one preliminary question, then move to 10 core questions about your attention, state, and habits. Choose the option that resonates most with your experience.
t.q0.text: Do you have diagnosed or noticeable nervous system impairments?
t.q0.a3: Yes, I have a diagnosis or am recovering from an injury or stroke
t.q1.text: How do you typically feel throughout an ordinary day?
t.q1.a1: Composed, stable, with clear sense of direction
t.q1.a2: Generally fine, but I often get tired and lose focus
t.q1.a3: Often overwhelmed, anxious, or emotionally unstable
t.q1.a4: I feel like I'm living in constant stress or burnout
t.q2.text: What typically happens with your attention?
t.q2.a1: I can direct and sustain it fairly easily
t.q2.a2: I can hold attention, but I'm often distracted by my phone, thoughts, or tasks
t.q2.a3: My attention jumps around, I struggle to finish what I start
t.q2.a4: I barely manage my attention — it's constantly captured by external stimuli
t.q3.text: How do you respond to change, uncertainty, and new circumstances?
t.q3.a1: I enjoy change and adapt quickly
t.q3.a2: I adapt, but it takes a lot of energy
t.q3.a3: Change often throws me off balance
t.q3.a4: Changes trigger anxiety, resistance, or a sense of losing control
t.q4.text: How connected do you feel to your body?
t.q4.a1: I feel my body well, clearly recognize all internal signals and don't ignore them
t.q4.a2: I feel my body, but I often overlook these sensations
t.q4.a3: I feel disconnected from my body, I often live 'in my head'
t.q4.a4: I barely understand what's happening with my body and state
t.q5.text: What most often prevents you from living better?
t.q5.a1: Nothing stands in my way, I'm moving toward the next level of growth
t.q5.a2: Lack of energy, focus, and stable productivity
t.q5.a3: Anxiety, emotional ups and downs, feeling overwhelmed
t.q5.a4: Addictions, burnout, loss of interest, or feeling stuck
t.q6.text: How are you currently working with your state?
t.q6.a1: I already have practices, systems, or discipline
t.q6.a2: I practice sometimes, but not consistently
t.q6.a3: I understand something needs to happen, but I can't fit it into my life
t.q6.a4: I barely know how to regulate my state without external stimuli
t.q7.text: How do you interact with the flow of new technologies?
t.q7.a1: I actively use them consciously and to my advantage
t.q7.a2: I apply them, learn to integrate them, sometimes feel overwhelmed, but manage overall
t.q7.a3: I want to use them, but I often get lost in the information flow
t.q7.a4: It's difficult, I feel like I'm falling behind in many ways
t.q8.text: When you face difficulties, what usually happens?
t.q8.a1: I figure it out and find a solution
t.q8.a2: I understand the problem, but I don't always change the situation
t.q8.a3: I often get stuck in the same patterns
t.q8.a4: I feel like I don't understand how to change it
t.q9.text: Are you interested in helping others manage their state or life challenges?
t.q9.a2: I've thought about it sometimes, but haven't tried
t.q9.a3: Yes, I already help people or want to start
t.q9.a4: Very interested, I want to become a specialist or already work in this field
t.q10.text: Do you have support in your life (people, environment, purpose)?
t.q10.a1: Yes, I have quality stable support or environment
t.q10.a2: Yes, but it could be better, I'm working on improving its quality
t.q10.a4: Not really, I'm starting from zero.
t.rehab.title: Clarification
t.rehab.instruction: Please specify what's closest to your situation:
t.rehab.thanks: Thank you. Now let's move to the main 10 questions — they're the same for everyone.
t.result.message: Great! The test is complete. Your adaptation profile has been determined. See your results below.
t.result.existing: You've taken this test before. Would you like to view your results or retake the test?
t.result.retake.message: Perfect! Let's start. One preliminary question and 10 core questions — this will take a few minutes.
t.result.scores.title: Breakdown by category
t.result.rehab.text: A personalized recovery program with specialist support is recommended.
t.result.unlock.title: Unlock your full profile
t.profile.stable_explorer.name: STABLE EXPLORER
t.profile.stable_explorer.subtitle: Stable. Adapted. With potential for enhancement.
t.profile.stable_explorer.r1:
You're someone who already maintains basic stability in a rapidly changing world.
You don't get lost in the flow, but you're not yet using your full management potential.
t.profile.stable_explorer.l1:
Your main risk is staying at your current level.
The world is accelerating.
The gap between 'managing okay' and 'taking control' is becoming critical.
t.profile.stable_explorer.p1: With development, you can reach the next level, where:
t.profile.stable_explorer.p2: you manage your attention rather than just sustain it
t.profile.stable_explorer.p3: you shape your state instead of reacting to it
t.profile.stable_explorer.v1:
Your next step is
moving from adaptation to active management.
t.profile.high_load.name: HIGH-LOAD ADAPTER
t.profile.high_load.subtitle: Functioning. Holding load. Losing resources.
t.profile.high_load.r1:
You know how to handle pressure.
Even under stress, you keep moving.
But this happens at the cost of your internal resources.
t.profile.high_load.l1:
You live in constant resource depletion mode.
This leads to:
t.profile.high_load.p1: You can transition to a state where:
t.profile.high_load.p2: energy gets restored, not just spent
t.profile.high_load.v1:
Your next step is
stopping survival mode and learning to manage your energy.
t.profile.nervous.name: NERVOUS SYSTEM SENSITIVE
t.profile.nervous.subtitle: Overwhelm. Instability. Loss of grounding.
t.profile.nervous.r1:
Right now, your system is running at capacity.
Environmental changes overwhelm it faster than it can adapt.
t.profile.nervous.l1:
Your system is overloaded.
This shows up as:
t.profile.nervous.p1: With the right work, you can:
t.profile.nervous.p3: reach a new level of sensitivity and control
t.profile.nervous.v1:
Your next step is
restoring basic stability and grounding.
t.profile.fragmented.name: FRAGMENTED FOCUS
t.profile.fragmented.subtitle: Scattered attention. Loss of control.
t.profile.fragmented.r1: You're in an environment that captures your attention faster than you can hold it.
t.profile.fragmented.l1:
Your attention doesn't fully belong to you.
This leads to:
t.profile.fragmented.v1:
Your next step is
reclaiming control over attention as your core function.
t.profile.specialist.name: FUTURE SPECIALIST
t.profile.specialist.subtitle: Growth. Interest. Potential for impact.
t.profile.specialist.r1:
You're already moving beyond working only on yourself.
You have interest in understanding people and processes more deeply.
t.profile.specialist.p1: You can move to a level where you understand:
t.profile.specialist.v1:
Your next step is
moving from interest to systematic understanding.
